name: Team Member
description: Memory structure for developers working on a team. Covers your role within the team, shared coding conventions, project goals, and team resources.

memories.name: User Role
memories.description: Your identity, role on the team, and areas of ownership
content_prompts: What is your name, title, and which team are you on?
What areas of the codebase or product do you own or primarily contribute to?
Who do you report to and who are your closest collaborators?
What is your typical workflow (PR reviews, pair programming, async communication)?
memories.example_content:
---
type: user
name: User Role
description: Team member identity, ownership areas, and collaboration patterns
---

- Name: Alex Chen, Senior Backend Engineer on the Payments team
- Owns: payment processing pipeline, webhook delivery system
- Reports to: Sarah (Eng Manager), works closely with Maria (frontend) and Dev (SRE)
- Workflow: async-first, PRs require 1 approval, weekly syncs on Tuesdays

memories.name: Team Conventions
memories.description: Shared coding standards, PR process, and team-specific patterns everyone follows
content_prompts: What coding standards does your team enforce (linting rules, formatting, naming)?
What is your PR/code review process and what do reviewers look for?
Are there team-specific architectural patterns or abstractions you use?
What branching strategy does the team use (trunk-based, gitflow, etc.)?
Are there any team-specific testing requirements (coverage thresholds, integration tests)?
memories.example_content:
---
type: feedback
name: Team Conventions
description: Shared standards and processes the team follows for consistent code
---

- Linting: ESLint with team config, Prettier with 100 char line width
- PRs: must include tests, description template required, 1 approval minimum
- Architecture: repository pattern for data access, domain events for cross-service communication
- Branching: trunk-based with short-lived feature branches, squash merge
- Testing: 80% coverage minimum, integration tests for all API endpoints

memories.name: Project Goals
memories.description: Current team objectives, sprint goals, and technical roadmap items
content_prompts: What is the team's current quarterly objective or OKR?
What are the active sprint or iteration goals?
Are there any upcoming deadlines or launches the team is working toward?
What technical initiatives are in progress (migrations, refactors, new features)?
memories.example_content:
---
type: project
name: Project Goals
description: Team objectives, active sprint goals, and upcoming milestones
---

- Q1 OKR: Reduce payment failure rate from 3.2% to under 1%
- Sprint goal: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for failed charges
- Upcoming: PCI compliance audit in March — all logging changes must be done by Feb 28
- Tech initiative: migrating from REST webhooks to event-driven architecture

memories.name: Team Resources
memories.description: Links to team docs, dashboards, runbooks, and communication channels
content_prompts: Where does the team keep its documentation (wiki, Notion, Confluence)?
What monitoring dashboards or alerting tools does the team use?
Where are the team's runbooks for incident response?
What communication channels does the team use (Slack channels, email lists)?
memories.example_content:
---
type: reference
name: Team Resources
description: Links to team documentation, dashboards, runbooks, and communication channels
---

- Docs: Notion workspace at notion.so/payments-team
- Dashboards: Grafana payment-health dashboard, Datadog APM
- Runbooks: GitHub wiki in payments-service repo
- Channels: #payments-eng (Slack), payments-eng@company.com
